issues.comment: TopicUser.find_by returns nil when no record exists (e.g., a user clicking the unsubscribe link for a topic they've never visited). Calling tu.notification_level on nil will raise NoMethodError: undefined method 'notification_level' for nil:NilClass, resulting in a 500 error.

The computed property is named stopNotificiationsText with an extra 'i' making it 'Notificiations' instead of 'Notifications'. While the controller and template are consistent with each other so it renders at runtime, this is a naming typo that will cause confusion during maintenance and won't match if anyone references the correctly-spelled name stopNotificationsText.

The template references stopNotificiationsText with the same typo ('Notificiations' instead of 'Notifications'). While consistent with the controller's misspelling so it works at runtime, this propagates the naming bug and will break if either side is corrected independently.
